DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: One valve in each main tank may be inoperative provided:

ListItem.Para: Inoperative valve is locked closed.
For inboard refuel valve inoperative, crossfeed valve is verified to operate normally before each departure.
For inboard refuel valve inoperative, operations procedure is used for fuel balancing.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Lock the inoperative main tank refuel valve closed and verify crossfeed valve operates normally (AMM DDG 28-21-01).

ListItem.Para: Lock the inoperative refuel valve closed.
For left main tank inboard refuel valve inoperative, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag:
REFUEL VLV CLOSE-LIB
REFUEL VLV OPEN-LIB
For left main tank outboard refuel valve inoperative, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag:
REFUEL VLV CLOSE-LOB
REFUEL VLV OPEN-LOB
For right main tank inboard refuel valve inoperative, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag:
REFUEL VLV CLOSE-RIB
REFUEL VLV OPEN-RIB
For right main tank outboard refuel valve inoperative, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag:
REFUEL VLV CLOSE-ROB
REFUEL VLV OPEN-ROB
Gain access to the inoperative refuel valve.
Disconnect, cap and stow the valve electrical connector.
Lock the valve closed.
For inboard refuel valve inoperative, verify crossfeed valve operates normally. (Required by AD 2019-19-10)
Gain access to the crossfeed valve in the main gear wheel well.
Set Fuel Control Panel (P5) CROSSFEED switch to ON and visually confirm the valve drive moves from the closed (C) position to the open (O) position.
Set Fuel Control Panel (P5) CROSSFEED switch to OFF and visually confirm the valve drive moves from the open (O) position to closed (C) position.

Note.Para: For outboard refuel valve inoperative, fueling time will significantly increase.
For inboard refuel valve inoperative, the fuel balance system will be inoperative.

ListItem.Para: For inboard refuel valve inoperative, prior to each flight, verify crossfeed valve operates normally.
Set Fuel Control Panel (P5) CROSSFEED switch to ON and after 15-seconds confirm FUEL CROSSFEED advisory message does not display.
Set Fuel Control Panel (P5) CROSSFEED switch to OFF and after 15-seconds confirm FUEL CROSSFEED advisory message does not display.
For fuel balancing with inboard refuel valve inoperative, do the FUEL BALANCE SYS Non-Normal Checklist.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Lock the inoperative center tank refuel valve closed (AMM DDG 28-21-01).

ListItem.Para: For center left tank refuel valve inoperative, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag:
REFUEL VLV CLOSE-C L
REFUEL VLV OPEN-C L
For center right tank refuel valve inoperative, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag:
REFUEL VLV CLOSE-C R
REFUEL VLV OPEN-C R
Open and deactivate the landing gear doors.
Gain access to the inoperative center tank refuel valve in the main gear wheel well.
Disconnect, cap and stow the valve electrical connector.
Lock the valve closed.
Close the landing gear doors.
Associated valve is verified closed after each refueling.
Overfill light operates normally.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Verify the associated valve is closed after each refueling (AMM DDG 28-21-01).

ListItem.Para: After each refueling, confirm the associated REFUEL VALVE status message is not displayed.

MELSubItem.Title: Refuel Control Panel Fuel Quantity Indicators

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are used for refueling.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Verify the fuel quantity in the tank associated with the inoperative quantity indication using one of the following methods (AMM DDG 28-21-01):

ListItem.Para: Refuel using the Refuel Control Panel (RCP) (P28) appropriate Load Select Display fuel quantity indication (if operating).
Refuel using the flight deck EICAS or Fuel Synoptic quantity indications (if operating).

Determine the associated tank quantity using the FMC calculated Fuel on CDU PROGRESS page from the previous flight and the operating fuel quantity indications. Then refuel the associated tank with a known quantity of fuel.
Defuel the associated tank and refuel with a known quantity of fuel.

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: May be inoperative provided maintenance procedure is used for refueling.

Note.Para: Any function which operates normally may be used.
The Load Select System includes the Load Select Displays, the Display Select switches, the Load Select Quantity switch and the All Valves switch.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Use alternate procedures for refueling (AMM DDG 28-21-01).

ListItem.Para: Utilize individual components of pressure refueling system which operate to refuel.
Refuel valves are verified to close when appropriate during refueling.
Refuel valve lights operate normally.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Verify refuel valves close when appropriate during refueling (AMM DDG 28-21-01).

ListItem.Para: Monitor and ensure the refuel valves close appropriately during refueling.

MaintenanceNote.Para: Refueling using battery mode will not be available.

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: One or both may be inoperative.

ListItem.Para: Load select system operates normally.
Overfill light operates normally.
